title: phleum alpinum proles commutatum
intro: Phleum alpinum proles commutatum, commonly known as alpine timothy grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Phleum alpinum. It is a type of grass that grows in high-altitude regions, particularly in the mountainous areas of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Alpine timothy grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and coughs, and to promote overall well-being.
